notes.content:
FB ad submission 
Describe what is happening:
I need a complete re-wire. Battery cables from main computer, battery cables from battery to battery. Wiring harness, wiring, for Speakers, lights, and any other electronics.
I also need some front end work done. It looks like the rubber bushing connecting the shocks to the wheels are worn out/gone....
I do have some parts for the front end I purchased.
Was going to eventually do myself. Same for wiring.
Overwhelming project that I don't have time to do. I have 6 - 8V batteries that should be in decent condition. Just need rewiring and front end work.



Had gotten new batteries. Main terminals that go to batteries and inter connect cables from battery to battery. Wiring harness for radio and lights.   Did have lights under the cart. Wants to keep and make sure they are working. 

The rubber bushing that connect to the bottom of the shock The rubber is gone

When was this implemented: "This fee is credited toward the total cost of authorized repairs. "...Diagnostics should be a standalone charge and not credited or removed if the root cause is subsequently worked on...don't understand why this statement is in this ticket. Any Time = Cost and Revenue Required

FRED: It was determined that this is a project that we have to see in person to figure out what to charge for. The minimum is to guarantee that we get paid for bringing it in. This is not a diagnostic fee. If it needs diagnostics, we will let them know and get it approved separately.

PRIOR to "Jump Starting" any LFP Battery, one should always wake the BMS first, evaluate, document findings on TEMP and individual Cell Voltages, then if OK - resuscitate. Should NOT be jumping or forcing a charge on a LFP Pack with the BMS hibernating or sleeping as that can cause irreversible damage.

notes.content: Good Diag with the Handheld, but need to be using 2.5A to 5A FUSED Jumpers please. notes.content: The Motor Controller was sent of to FSIP for Analysis. FSIP was unable to determine any faults, but agreed to send a different Controller back with Speed Code 4 present on 2/27/26. The Tech is to replace the Controller and install a new OE Main Contactor at the same time prior to test driving the cart. Tech should also record a new Log Report during the initial Test Run and operate through several full stop (solenoid off reset) and goes, also measured throttle and WOT.

notes.content:
BMS Faults reviewed and noted as:
1) Single Cell - MIN Cell Voltages was less than 2.5V at Wakeup (79)
2) Cell Undervoltage Warning 1 (54)
3) Cell Undervoltage Protection 1 (4)

If issues with charging continue, the Modules would need inspected with a Special BMS Reading Tool called a Battery Diagnostic Tool (BDT) which is priced at over $1700 coupled with the Pack Diagnostics APP.

jobs.description: 1.0 - Golf Cart Diagnostic & Inspection [Mobile Service Call]
1.0 - Golf Cart Diagnostic & Inspection [Mobile Service Call]

notes.content:
Consulted with Tech while onsite. Tech stated that the Battery Switch was ON / Green Light upon arrival and was unable to communicate to the APP. This usually represents a battery pack voltage less than 7-10V as that is the minimum for a BMS to wake up via a Dongle interrogation. I suggested that all leads from B- be removed, the RS485 plug be disconnected at the Battery and the Battery SW be turned OFF. Sometimes, the battery can recover on its own as the Temps increase - other times it may need a little assistance using a controller voltage and current via Power Supply via the RS485 B+ and B- circuit to wake up the BMS to determine cell voltages and condition. Any reviving of the Battery should be monitored, in a controlled environment and documented prior to returning to service. NOTE: If the cells are determined to be less than 1.0V - they can be considered Bricked and should not be revived.
//

1.0 - Golf Cart Diagnostic & Inspection [Mobile Service Call]

notes.content: USA Batteries - Use caution - Appears to be missing pre-charge resistor and diode on Solenoid. The FnR ASM appears miswired too. Uploaded wire diagram for this cart in attachments.
Almost appears that the A-ARM Bolt to Strut Flange is lose - possible missing cover thrust washers and/or worn A-ARM Bushings.

Replaced wheel bearings and lug nuts. It appears we had a bad set of wheel bearings installed or they were tightened too much upon install causing premature wear. 
Customer test drove and confirmed cart is fixed.

notes.content: The Motor Controller was sent of to FSIP for Analysis. FSIP was unable to determine any faults, but agreed to send a different Controller back with Speed Code 4 present on 2/27/26. The Tech is to replace the Controller and install a new OE Main Contactor at the same time prior to test driving the cart. Tech should also record a new Log Report during the initial Test Run and operate through several full stop (solenoid off reset) and goes, also measured throttle and WOT.

jobs.description: 1.0 - Golf Cart Diagnostic & Inspection [Mobile Service Call]

notes.content: Battery estimate.

1/29/26 - It was determined that this cart has a TPS, not an MCOR installed on the Go Pedal. It was also determined that original tech data may not have been available to Tech to T/S using Pin Point Testing as the Handheld was reported to be not communicating to the Controller. This job needs re-T/S to determine if the Controller is receiving good PWR and GNDS and Pin Point Testing at the Controller Connector for Throttle input is needed. Also need to inspect and bench test the Main Contactor/Solenoid to ensure proper operation. If all appears OK to the Controller, perform a Motor Checkout using Continuity Checks and Megger first then attempt to operate isolated with a 12V source prior to condemning the Controller.

Retrieved controller on 2/11 to send off for rebuild. 
Model: 1515-5201
SN: 08256P.510456

notes.content: What can we do for you?: My cart won’t run!

Performed Diag on a DC House Battery with Ryan. The DSG FET would not respond initially to the BAT SW. Once we cycled the FETs, then BAT SW operated. When charging was attempted, Ryan stated would only ramp up to 5A and was draining the battery - not charging it per an external AMP clamp. Advised to isolate and secure the battery - needs returned for BMS issue.//FT

Pedal would be pushed all the way down and the speed would very. It has now stopped working completely. Maybe a 3 year old cart.

customer.notes: . The wire was behind the instrument panel and came from the ignition/ key switch. I taped it up and reinstalled the panel and key switch. The unit is 72V model and has fairly new batteries, I think they are less than two years old. The cart doesn't get used very often only to run around in the woods on the farm, which is usually once or twice a month. I couldn’t find any date plates on the frame. They did have a sticker on the steering shaft one on the motors. I'll look again in the morning.
